name: Rice with Potatoes, Cilantro and Mint
recipeIngredient: 2 cups basmati rice, or other long-grain rice -- dry measure
recipeInstructions:
PREPARATION (20 minutes)
1. Soak the saffron in a small bowl with 1 tablespoon of the boiling water for at least 10 minutes.
2. Wash the rice under cold running water until the water runs clear. Drain, then place it in a medium-sized bowl, cover with water and soak for 30 minutes.
COOKING (25 minutes)
3. Bring a large saucepan of water to a boil. Add the rice, stir well, and boil rapidly for 5 minutes. Drain.
4. While the rice is cooking, heat the oil in a large, flameproof casserole dish over medium heat. Add the cloves and cinnamon and stir for 30 seconds. Add the potatoes and cook, stirring frequently, until golden, about 5 minutes. Remove from the heat.
5. Sprinkle the mint and half of the ginger over the potato mixture.
6. Spread half of the rice over the potato mixture.
7. Place the yogurt, salt, saffron, and saffron liquid in a small bowl. Combine well. Pour half of the yogurt mixture over the rice in the casserole dish.
8. Sprinkle the remaining ginger, the cilantro, and onion on top and cover with the remaining rice. Pour the remaining yogurt mixture over the rice.
9. Pour the remaining boiling water down the side of the mixture. Cover and bake in the oven until the rice is tender and all of the liquid is absorbed, about 15 minutes.
10. Loosen the edges of the cake from the casserole dish. Warm the serving plate. Put the platter on top of the casserole and turn off the rice onto the plate.
